notes: SHIP v1.14.244 — MONOLITH PLATE: Forge nameplate redesign (VISUAL; one function + one helper inside the FORGE HYBRID AISLE module; per HANDOFF-FORGE-MONOLITH-PLATE.md, John device-approved mock variant D 2026-07-13). No geometry, no slot logic, no data, NO CSS. WHY: the 3D racks read as machined objects; the name tile above them read as a whiteboard rectangle (flat fill + 3px strokeRect + one line of text, 256x64). John's field call: 'basic and blah.' NOW: a machined gunmetal plaque — brushed grain (sin-deterministic), chamfered slab, dark/light bevel pair, four corner bolts, ENGRAVED ID (dual-pass emboss: dark cut + light rim + steel-gradient face), and a routed light channel that IGNITES cyan on focus. The plaque belongs to the same material world as the racks — that is the entire thesis. Canvas 256x64 -> 576x144 (2x res, SAME 4:1 plane: PlaneGeometry(1.5, 0.375) UNTOUCHED), so every plate in the scene also gets sharper. Anisotropy applied (gut-texture precedent) so engraved type stays crisp at walk angles. CONTRACT PRESERVED EXACTLY — (text, focus) -> Mesh{PlaneGeometry 1.5x0.375, MeshBasicMaterial{map: CanvasTexture}, toneMapped:false} — so the dispose paths in BOTH callers that recycle plates (assignSlot: old.material.map/material/geometry; setFocus: same) keep working with zero change. ⚠️ DEVIATION FROM SPEC — §3 FONT RE-BAKE HOOK DROPPED (evidence, not preference). The spec adds a document.fonts.ready one-shot re-bake to defend against a first-paint font race freezing a fallback font into the plaque. VERIFIED AGAINST LIVE SOURCE: this app ships NO webfont for Orbitron or Chakra Petch — the ONLY @font-face is 'PhantomBrand', there are ZERO fonts.googleapis/gstatic links (the offline-first no-CDN guard), and --orb/--raj resolve to '-apple-system, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif'. Those two family names therefore ALWAYS fall back to their generic (sans-serif / monospace) — exactly as the OLD plate's 'Chakra Petch' already did for the last several ships. There is no font to race, so the hook is a NO-OP. Worse, as specced it would have INTRODUCED TWO BUGS: (1) it re-bakes via assignSlot(s, label), which calls plate(label, FALSE) — that would strip the lit channel off the CURRENTLY FOCUSED rack, and setFocus early-returns on (focused === rack) so nothing restores it until the user taps away; (2) it iterates only `focusables`, which holds just the five 'run' racks — the 'dummy' neighbour racks built at L18075 ALSO carry plates and would never re-bake. Shipping a no-op that breaks focus state is strictly worse than shipping nothing. If a real webfont is ever embedded, THAT is when a re-bake is needed — condition + correct implementation recorded in INTEGRATION-STATE §15. VERIFIED (§4 guards, real browser, shipped plate()/plateChamfer extracted verbatim and run against a live canvas with THREE stubbed): DETERMINISTIC — two consecutive plate('dh1:005', true) canvases are pixel-identical (recycled slots never shimmer). CONTRACT — all 7 cases return 576x144 canvas, plane 1.5x0.375, CanvasTexture, toneMapped false, anisotropy 16. FOCUS — focused plates measure ~12.2% cyan-dominant pixels; EVERY idle plate measures cyan = 0.0000 (etched, legible, NO glow — the shot that matters, since idle is 80% of what is on screen). PAD ('· · ·') is the quietest surface (ink 0.009). NO-COLON DEFENSIVENESS — 'R42' (ink 0.027) and the 'F-06' dummy-rack labels (ink 0.029) render CENTERED at full size, never blank. Real ids confirmed: dh1:005, c1:002 (DFW2), s3:175 (SPARKS). Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js, CSS braces 3856/3856 (IDENTICAL — this ship adds no CSS), CRLF preserved, three-stamp lockstep .243 -> .244. ?legacy=1: unaffected — #forge3d-sheet is CSS-gated to body.rd ('body.rd #forge3d-sheet.open{display:flex}'), so legacy cannot render the Forge scene at all; and this ship changes only pixels INSIDE plate(), touching no gating, no markup and no shared code. ⚠️ BATCH = 7 (.238 .239 .240 .241 .242 .243 .244) — ONE PAST THE 6-CAP. John was told the batch was at cap and shipped this anyway; flagged here per CALL 0. Device pass now owes: DFW2 c1:002 + SPARKS s3:175 (heights) · the four SCOPE FLOW guards (.243) · and Forge: focused plate lit / idle plates legible WITHOUT glow / NEXT-PREV walk with no recycle hitch / oblique-angle sharpness / cold PWA reopen.
